curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I threw a television out of a hotel window once and the bastard landed on a room-service cart. Beef Wellington everywhere. Housekeeping sent me a bill. I sent them a signed drum skin. We called it even.
quotes.speaker: John Bonham
quotes.context: Circus magazine, backstage interview at Madison Square Garden, June 1977
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bonham's rep for hotel destruction is legendary and the beef Wellington detail feels perfectly absurd and on-brand. The giveaway is 'signed drum skin' as currency; too tidy and self-mythologising even for Bonham.

quotes.text: Look at your mushrooms - they look like fucking tadpoles on Viagra.
quotes.speaker: Gordon Ramsay
quotes.context: Chef Gordon Ramsay critiquing a kitchen's mushroom side dish on the "Rococo" episode of Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares (King's Lynn restaurant), UK Channel 4, 2006.

quotes.text: I'd sit there and smoke crack while I listened to the play. It made me fucking crazy.
quotes.speaker: Samuel L. Jackson
quotes.context: Reflecting on his 1980s/early-90s crack-cocaine addiction during his time as an understudy on Broadway's 'The Piano Lesson' (shadowing Charles Dutton), before he got sober in 1991. From a 2019 The Hollywood Reporter interview, widely reported (Entertainment Tonight, Today, People), Jan 2019.

quotes.text: My wife asked me what I find attractive. I said, 'Honestly? A woman who laughs at my jokes and doesn't check my credit score.' She said, 'So literally anyone but me.' I said, 'Baby, you got two out of three.'
quotes.speaker: Rodney Dangerfield
quotes.context: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson, NBC, recorded November 1981
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Dangerfield's entire persona was self-deprecating marriage grief and the punchline structure is pure him. The giveaway is 'credit score' — that phrasing wasn't really everyday slang in his prime TV years.

quotes.text: It's cold outside, but I'm still looking like a thotty, because a hoe never gets cold.
quotes.speaker: Cardi B
quotes.context: From a now-iconic 2014 viral Instagram/Vine clip from her pre-fame days, strutting in a bra top and skirt and explaining why she's barely dressed in winter.

quotes.text: I did a gig in Cleveland once — and look, I love Cleveland, mostly — but a man in the third row fell asleep during my set, and I thought, either I'm off tonight or this man is genuinely broken. Turns out both. We went for drinks after. Great guy.
quotes.speaker: Bill Hicks
quotes.context: Recorded Q&A at the Laff Stop, Austin, Texas, February 1992
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Hicks's love-hate relationship with mid-America crowds is well documented and the ending redemption twist is his style. The giveaway is 'we went for drinks after' — Hicks would more likely have ended on the misanthropy than a warm reunion.
